title: hearth.perf.slow_query v1
description: Emitted by hearth-api when a database query exceeds the slow-statement threshold (currently 1s). Produced from SQLx's log_slow_statements hook or from explicit instrumentation around expensive window-function queries. Used to surface DB hotspots to the Grafana sysmap dashboard without requiring log scraping.

query_summary.description: First 120 chars of the SQL statement or a human-readable label (e.g. 'get_recent_tech_news_balanced').

elapsed_ms.description: Observed query execution time in milliseconds.

endpoint.description: HTTP method + path that triggered the query, e.g. 'GET /api/news/digest'. Empty string if triggered from a background task.

rows_returned.description: Number of rows returned by the query. Helps distinguish slow-but-small from slow-and-large result sets.

ts.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp when the slow query completed.

table.description: Primary table name extracted from the query, if determinable. Optional.
